formations_blocks: Cape Verde in a four-at-the-back defensive shape
Uruguay playing with short passing and sharp movement in possession
Cape Verde effectively in a deeper defensive block under early pressure
key_matchups: Lopes Cabral repeatedly targeted in duels/fouls by Cano and others
Cano vs Borges in a physical early contest
phases.summary: Uruguay settled quickly into a controlled, short-passing attacking rhythm, repeatedly progressing down the left and forcing early corners. Cape Verde were pinned back in a four-at-the-back setup and struggled to clear their lines, while the match became increasingly physical around Lopes Cabral, who was repeatedly fouled and then booked.

formations_blocks: Uruguay in sustained attacking pressure / probing possession
Cape Verde defending in a compact low block
Cape Verde quick-direct counterattacking phase after winning fouls
adjustments: Uruguay slowed the tempo after the opening pressure
Cape Verde repeatedly used direct long distribution from Vozinha
Cape Verde’s free-kick set piece exploited a gap in Uruguay’s wall
phases.summary: Uruguay started the phase on the front foot, but Cape Verde settled into a compact defensive shape and made the match more physical, repeatedly drawing fouls and then using direct play to relieve pressure. The key turning point was Pina’s 21' free-kick goal through a gap in the wall, after which Uruguay’s pressure remained largely sterile until they equalized late in the half with a goal from Araújo at 44'.

formations_blocks: recycled attacks from midfield into the final third
left-flank build-up near the goal line
adjustments: Uruguay introduced Cano and Sanabria compared with the opening game
Halftime substitution: Arcanjo replaced by Duarte for Cape Verde
Uruguay capitalized on a quick restart/transition after Muslera collected the ball
Cape Verde’s partial clearance was recycled into a second-phase attack
key_matchups: Cano vs Cape Verde defenders, with Lopes keeping him onside for the 2-1 goal
Bentancur between two defenders on Uruguay’s equalizer buildup
Borges vs Uruguayan defenders before Muslera claimed the shot
phases.summary: Uruguay went into halftime 2-1 up, with the key tactical theme being their ability to punish moments when Cape Verde switched off and failed to control restarts or second balls. The goals came from a fast transition after a save/collection and from a recycled attack after a partial clearance, while Cape Verde’s opener stemmed from a set-piece situation that the commentary explicitly frames as an outlier relative to the run of play.

formations_blocks: Uruguay sustained long possession in Cape Verde’s half, effectively a patient territorial siege.
Cape Verde responded with pressing spells to disrupt Uruguay’s circulation and force turnovers.
Cape Verde then settled into a more compact defensive stance to handle Uruguay’s corners and keep the game contained.
adjustments: Uruguay continued to probe with diagonal balls and right-sided deliveries after the interval.
Cape Verde increased their press around the 56th minute, forcing Muslera into an error and briefly winning field position.
Benchimol and Rodrigues were substituted off for Da Costa and Varela before the free kick at 58', altering Cape Verde’s personnel in attack/forward areas.
key_matchups: Viñas vs a Cape Verde defender on the 45'+4 penalty-area run
Lopes Cabral vs Muslera on the 56' pressing sequence
Benchimol vs Olivera on the 58' turn and shirt pull
phases.summary: The second-half phase was dominated by Uruguay’s long possession and repeated entries into Cape Verde’s half, with the hosts still looking for openings through diagonal balls and territorial pressure. Cape Verde briefly improved their press to force an error from Muslera, while their own attacking moments remained limited and often came from direct shots or brief transitions. The only notable tactical shift in the text is Cape Verde’s substitution of Benchimol and Rodrigues for Da Costa and Varela, made around the moment they won a dangerous free kick.

formations_blocks: Uruguay controlling possession before the game opens up
Cape Verde hanging on in a defensive shape
Uruguay attacking via corners and a dangerous free kick
adjustments: Cape Verde used their final substitution at 80' with Monteiro off and Semedo on
Uruguay changed personnel at 81' with Araújo off and Rodriguez on
Uruguay’s keeper Muslera continues to act as a sweeper despite the risk highlighted by the earlier error
key_matchups: De La Cruz vs the Cape Verde defender who trips him for the late free kick
Uruguay corners vs Cape Verde’s box defending
Vozinha vs Uruguay’s late pressure and crosses
phases.summary: The match shifts from Uruguay control to an open, frantic finish after a goalkeeper error and a defensive lapse, with Cape Verde briefly pressuring and intercepting but struggling to build possession. Uruguay regain some control late through corners, wide deliveries, and a dangerous free kick, while Cape Verde are forced deeper and appear to be hanging on under sustained pressure.
